old_claim.note: Herbalism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct herbalism dependency remains because the node's source-backed anchor is pigment compound processing, not medicinal plant selection or therapeutic practice.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: the Science paper reports a 100,000-year-old ochre-processing workshop involving pigment-rich compound production.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports early material processing through ochre compounds, not herbal medicine as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Herbalism remains a medical and botanical neighbor; retaining it direct would blend therapeutic practice into a material-processing node.

ontology_before: Early Alchemy & Material Science depended on Herbalism as an unsourced enabling edge.
ontology_after: Early Alchemy & Material Science keeps Pigment Creation as the direct source-backed material transformation prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: herbalism is no longer a direct prerequisite for early_alchemy_material_science.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to medicinal preparations rather than broad material transformation.
A stronger source showed herbalism directly enabled the source-backed ochre compound process.
A separate early_pharmacological_compounding node were added with herbalism as its direct scope.
short_reason: Herbalism is medical context, not the material-processing anchor.
